title: Implement verification module lane

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the full #1159 verification lane (#1160-#1165) on this branch.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Preserve the reviewed owner boundary: Verification owns reusable protocols and bounded evidence, not Assurance requirements, Planning active work, Proof authority, Closeout claims, Memory, CI, or raw transcript storage.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Concrete schema shape, report/proof projection details, tests, and docs needed to satisfy the lane while keeping ordinary tiny paths quiet.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Finish validation for the verification manifest/report/proof/closeout implementation.
proof_expectations: Focused report and proof tests for verification behavior pass.
Ruff passes on changed source and tests.
Workspace proof-selected commands are run or any broader test not run is reported.
completion_criteria: #1160: verification manifest is parsed and projected through report.
#1161: matched protocols route into proof and routine context.
#1162: bounded evidence bundles are projected and closeout can cite them.
#1163: transcript retention/summarization policy is documented and represented.
#1164: assurance requirements can map to verification protocols.
#1165: tests cover manual/non-code and high-assurance/long-horizon evidence workflows.

goal: Implement the full #1159 verification lane (#1160-#1165) on this branch.
non_goals: Do not build a separate package.
Do not add a generic QA management system, CI runner, raw transcript database, or broad compliance interpreter.
Do not move Assurance, Planning, Proof, Closeout, or Memory ownership into Verification.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace runtime/reporting source, focused report/proof tests, verification/routine docs, and planning evidence for this lane.

execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from verification manifest/report/proof/closeout implementation and run selected validation.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.
